Curse.spell_requirement: Illusion spell known by the ritual leader
Curse.description: Allows the ritual team to cast an Illusion spell on a subject through a material link instead of establishing a mystic link by observing the subject. The target can be anywhere, provided the ritual has a usable material link to that target. If the chosen spell affects multiple targets, the ritual requires a material link to each one. The material link is used as part of the offering and is effectively destroyed during the ritual. The spell is otherwise cast normally, including its usual tests and Drain. While the spell is sustained, a link remains between the target and ritual group that can potentially be detected and traced back to the ritual team's physical location.

Prodigal Spell.spell_requirement: Combat spell known by the ritual leader
Prodigal Spell.description: Allows the ritual team to cast a Combat spell at a distant target outside the team's line of sight. A spotter must establish the mystic link to the target. The chosen Combat spell may be direct or indirect. A direct Combat spell travels to the target through astral space. An indirect Combat spell travels through physical space and therefore requires a clear, though not necessarily straight, physical path between the ritual team and the target; the spell travels from the foundation to the target while avoiding obstacles along that path.

Remote Sensing.spell_requirement: Detection spell known by the ritual leader
Remote Sensing.area: Force × total Magic of all participants × 100 meters
Remote Sensing.description: Allows the ritual team to cast a Detection spell with greatly increased range. The subject of the spell must be present in the ritual foundation during the ritual, but may leave afterward and continue using the new sense while the participants sustain it. Every participant perceives the same thing the subject perceives through the spell. If the chosen Detection spell has a separate target, such as Mind Link or Mind Probe, a spotter is required to establish the mystic link to that target. The ritual ends as soon as any participant stops sustaining it.

Ward.duration: Net hits on sealing test in weeks
Ward.area: Up to 50 cubic meters × total Magic of all participants
Ward.description: Creates an astral barrier with a Force equal to the ritual's Force. The ward may enclose a volume of up to 50 cubic meters multiplied by the sum of the participants' Magic ratings. It normally lasts for a number of weeks equal to the net hits from the sealing test. The ritual leader may make the ward permanent by spending Karma equal to the ward's Force.

Circle of Protection.duration: Net hits on sealing test in hours
Circle of Protection.area: Sphere with radius equal to the ritual leader's Magic rating in meters
Circle of Protection.description: Creates a spherical astral and physical barrier around the ritual's anchor. The circle functions as a combination of the Physical Barrier and Mana Barrier spells, each at a Force equal to the ritual's Force; the mana barrier is dual-natured. Anyone or anything inside the sphere is protected from external physical objects and magical attacks. The ritual ends immediately if something crosses the barrier from the inside.

Circle of Healing.spell_requirement: Healing magic known by the ritual leader
Circle of Healing.area: Sphere with radius equal to the ritual leader's Magic rating in meters
Circle of Healing.description: Allows healing magic to affect multiple targets within a spherical area around the ritual's anchor. The net hits from the sealing step are used as the net hits for the chosen healing spell and apply to everyone within the circle for as long as they remain inside it. The circle persists for a number of days equal to the ritual's Force.

Renascence.spell_requirement: Area Manipulation spell known by the ritual leader
Renascence.duration: 1 hour, doubled for each net hit on the sealing test
Renascence.area: Sphere with radius equal to the ritual leader's Magic rating in meters
Renascence.description: Sustains and maintains an area Manipulation spell for an extended period. The spell affects a spherical area around the anchor with a radius equal to the ritual leader's Magic rating in meters. The effect is determined by the ritual's Force and the net hits from the sealing test. Its base duration is 1 hour, doubled once for every net hit from the sealing step.

Watcher.duration: Force × net hits on sealing test in hours
Watcher.description: Creates a watcher: a temporary astral entity formed from mana and the personalities of the ritual participants. A watcher can speak every language known by its creators, is bound only to the ritual leader, and follows the leader's orders. Its skill ratings equal half the ritual's Force, rounded up. The leader may dismiss it as a Free Action. Watchers cannot be banished or counterspelled.

notes: Watchers do not have corporeal bodies.
Watcher skill ratings equal half the ritual's Force, rounded up.

Homunculus.duration: Force × net hits on sealing test in days
Homunculus.description: Binds a spark of intelligence into an inanimate object to create a physical servant. The chosen object may weigh no more than Force × 10 kilograms and gains limited flexibility equivalent to the Animate spell for the ritual's duration. The object's Object Resistance dice pool is added to the opposition for the ritual team's Ritual Spellcasting Test. The homunculus understands every language spoken by its creators and reflects some of their personality, but it is far less intelligent and intuitive than a spirit. It follows the ritual leader's commands as well as its physical capabilities allow.

stats.body: Structure rating of the material used to create the body

category.Rituals:
Ritual Spellcasting
Rituals shape mana over a longer period than ordinary spellcasting, allowing effects that normal spellcasting cannot achieve. Each ritual is learned separately and costs the same Karma as learning a spell. A ritual may be performed by one magician or by a group.

Basic Procedure
  1. Choose a ritual leader who knows the ritual.
  2. Choose the ritual and, where required, the spell incorporated into it.
  3. Choose the ritual's Force.
  4. Establish a ritual foundation using an appropriate magical lodge whose Force equals or exceeds the ritual's Force.
  5. Provide any required offering or reagents.
  6. Perform the ritual.
  7. Seal the ritual with a Ritual Spellcasting + Magic [Force] v. Force × 2 test, using Teamwork Tests from the participants where applicable.
Drain
After the ritual resolves, every participant resists Drain equal to twice the hits scored by the opposing side of the sealing test, with a minimum Drain Value of 2. The Drain is Physical if the leader's hits on the Teamwork Test exceed the leader's Magic rating; otherwise it is Stun.

keywords.Anchored:
Anchored
The ritual must be attached to a physical object, mystical symbol, inscription, or the ritual foundation itself. The anchor cannot move relative to the Gaiasphere while the ritual remains active; moving it causes the ritual to collapse.
keywords.Material Link:
Material Link
The ritual requires something that was once physically part of the target. Material links taken from living beings lose viability as the target's aura fades from them, though freezing can preserve a sample for later use.
keywords.Minion:
Minion
The ritual creates a semi-autonomous entity bound to the ritual leader. A ritual leader cannot have more active minions at one time than their Charisma rating.
keywords.Spell:
Spell
The ritual incorporates a spell known by the ritual leader. The ritual description determines how the spell is used or modified. Mentor-spirit modifiers that apply to the spell also apply to the ritual, and ongoing rituals with this keyword can be susceptible to dispelling.
keywords.Spotter:
Spotter
When the ritual leader cannot see the target, a spotter establishes the mystic link. The spotter must be a ritual participant or a spirit bound to a participant, must be capable of astrally perceiving the target, and begins inside the ritual foundation before travelling to a position where the target can be assensed. The spotter does not make a Teamwork Test but suffers the same Drain as the other participants. The astral link to the ritual group can potentially be detected and traced back to the team's physical location.
